commit.message:
feat: add Keep Screen Awake setting (#212)

* feat: add Keep Screen Awake setting using Screen Wake Lock API

Adds a 'Keep Screen Awake' toggle (on by default) under Settings > Timer.
When enabled, acquires a Screen Wake Lock while the timer is running to
prevent the screen from sleeping. The lock is released when the timer
stops or the app unmounts. Gracefully no-ops on unsupported browsers.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* style: fix Prettier formatting in useWakeLock

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: trigger audio from worker action messages instead of pre-scheduling (#211)

Audio was pre-scheduled on the Web Audio API hardware clock at phase
start, while visual flashes were triggered by the worker's action
messages. The two clocks (AudioContext.currentTime vs performance.now)
could drift, causing beeps to desync from the timer.

Now both audio and visual cues are triggered from the same source —
the worker's action messages — ensuring they stay in lockstep.

- Remove schedulePhaseActions/cancelAllScheduled from usePhaseRunner
- Play sounds immediately via getSoundPlayer() in the action handler
- Remove ~130 lines of dead pre-scheduling infrastructure from sounds.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs(azure): replace ARM template deployment with pure az CLI commands (#50700)

* docs(azure): replace ARM template deployment with pure az CLI commands

Rewrites the Azure install guide to use individual az CLI commands
instead of referencing ARM templates in infra/azure/templates/ (removed
upstream). Each Azure resource (NSG, VNet, subnets, VM, Bastion) is now
created with explicit az commands, preserving the same security posture
(Bastion-only SSH, no public IP, NSG hardening).

Also addresses BradGroux review feedback from #47898:
- Add cost considerations section (Bastion ~$140/mo, VM ~$55/mo)
- Add cleanup/teardown section (az group delete)
- Remove stale /install/azure/azure redirect from docs.json

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs(azure): split into multiple Steps blocks for richer TOC

Add Quick path and What you need sections. Split the single Steps
block into three (Configure deployment, Deploy Azure resources,
Install OpenClaw) so H2 headers appear in the Mintlify sidebar TOC.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs(azure): remove Quick path section

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs(azure): fix cost section LaTeX rendering, remove comparison

Escape dollar signs to prevent Mintlify LaTeX interpretation.
Also escape underscores in VM SKU name within bold text.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs(azure): add caveat that deallocated VM stops Gateway

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs(azure): simplify install step with clearer description

Download then run pattern (no sudo). Clarify that installer handles
Node LTS, dependencies, OpenClaw install, and onboarding wizard.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs(azure): add Bastion provisioning latency note

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs(azure): use deployment variables in cost and cleanup sections

Replace hardcoded rg-openclaw/vm-openclaw with variables in

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs(azure): fix formatting (oxfmt)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Implement 4 major features: Zoom/Pan, Height from Ground, Materials Checklist, Power & Condensate Lines

Feature 1 – Zoom/Pan:
- Mouse wheel zooms centred on cursor (0.15×–8× range)
- Middle-mouse drag pans the canvas
- getRawPos() returns raw pixels; getPos() returns world coords
- drawGrid() redrawn for world-space viewport at any pan/zoom
- render() wraps all drawing in ctx.save/translate(pan)/scale(zoom)/restore
- Zoom −/+/⊙1:1 buttons and % indicator in canvas status bar

Feature 2 – Height from Ground:
- indoorHeights[] and outdoorHeight added to app state + undo history
- calculateResults() adds Δh to horizontal pipe length → totalMeters
- Results panel shows (Δh X.X m) annotation when heights differ
- Dynamic height inputs in left sidebar via updateHeightUI()
- Height labels drawn on canvas under each unit when height > 0

Feature 3 – Materials/Works Checklist:
- materials object with checkboxes for Staffa u.e., Lavaggio impianto, Predisposizione
- Included in undo history; reset in clearAll()
- New section added to right sidebar

Feature 4 – Power & Condensate Lines:
- New tools: placeOutlet, placeDrain, drawPowerPipe, drawCondensaPipe
- Dashed power (orange #FF5722) and condensate (cyan #00BCD4) traces with WIP preview
- Wall-snapping placement icons via drawSpecialUnits()
- completePipe(), clearPipe(), syncCompletePipeBtn(), onDblClick(),
  onKeyDown() all updated for new tools
- calculatePowerCondensaResults() shown in results panel as ⚡ and 💧 rows
- Legend updated with new entries

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add zoom/pan, height from ground, materials checklist, and power/condensate lines

Feature 1 – Zoom/Pan on canvas:
- Wheel to zoom centred on cursor; middle-mouse drag to pan
- getPos() now returns world coords; getRawPos() returns raw screen coords
- drawGrid() redrawn in world-space for full viewport coverage at any zoom
- render() wraps all drawing in save/translate(panX,panY)/scale(zoom)/restore
- Zoom +/− and reset buttons added in canvas status bar (#zoom-level display)

Feature 2 – Height from ground:
- indoorHeights[] and outdoorHeight on app state; persisted in history
- calculateResults() adds heightDiff + totalMeters (horizontal metres + Δh)
- Results panel shows Δh annotation; completePipe status uses totalMeters
- drawAcUnits() annotates each unit with its height when > 0
- updateHeightUI() dynamically builds number inputs (one per split + outdoor)

Feature 3 – Materials / Works checklist:
- materials: { staffaUE, lavaggioImpianto, predisposizione } on app state
- Checkboxes wired in init(); state saved/restored through undo; cleared in clearAll()
- New 'Materiali / Lavori' section in right sidebar

Feature 4 – Power line & Condensate line:
- New constants POWER_COLOR/DARK, CONDENSA_COLOR/DARK
- New tools: placeOutlet, placeDrain, drawPowerPipe, drawCondensaPipe
- powerPipe / condensatePipe completed paths; WIP arrays during drawing
- drawPowerCondensaPipes() renders dashed coloured lines with distance labels
- drawSpecialUnits() renders power outlet and condensate drain on walls
- calculatePowerCondensaResults() computes metres + wall crossings
- updateResults() appends ⚡ and 💧 rows; clearPipe/completePipe tool-aware
- All new state included in saveHistory/undo/loadTemplate/clearAll

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: live download clients, indexer search, JWT auth, release endpoints

Backend:
- Real qBittorrent WebUI v2 client (cookie-jar login, torrents add/list/remove)
- Real SABnzbd HTTP API client (addurl, queue, delete)
- Real NZBGet JSON-RPC client (appendurl, listgroups, editqueue)
- downloadclient.Build() registry factory wired to DB rows
- Queue grab endpoint dispatches AddTorrent/AddNZB to configured client
- Download client testOne makes live TestConnection() call
- Indexer testOne makes live /api?t=caps request
- /api/v1/release: search (parallel indexer fan-out), grab, list endpoints
- JWT auth: GenerateToken/ValidateToken, GenerateAPIKey
- Auth handler: POST /auth/login, GET /auth/me, POST /auth/logout, POST /auth/apikey/regenerate
- middleware.go: Bearer JWT accepted as alternative to API key
- DB migrations: 002_auth_sessions + 002_queue_release (download_url, releases cache table)

Frontend:
- Login page (dark theme, username+password form)
- ProtectedRoute wrapper (redirects to /login when unauthenticated)
- Zustand auth store persisted to localStorage
- axios interceptor: attaches Bearer token, redirects on 401
- App.tsx: wraps routes with ProtectedRoute, validates token on mount
- Settings: API key management with regenerate button
- Login test fixed: vi.spyOn + fireEvent, localStorage mock in setup.ts
- tsconfig: paths for lucide-react types (TS 5.9 bundler resolution fix)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[fix] Fix `custom_backend_meta` related issue (#47)

## Description
Running ```TQ_TEST_BACKEND=Yuanrong pytest -s
tests/e2e/test_e2e_lifecycle_consistency.py``` raise error.

## Main Changes
- Fix a bug where `custom_backend_meta` of `extended_meta` was missing
in
```test_e2e_lifecycle_consistency.py::test_cross_shard_complex_update```.
- Fix a bug where `KVStorageManager::put_data` used outdated
`field_names` of metadata.
- Add a preventative parameter checks to `KVStorageManager::put_data`.

## Other Infos
```bash
$ TQ_TEST_BACKEND=Yuanrong pytest -s tests/e2e/test_e2e_lifecycle_consistency.py

========================================================================================== short test summary info ===========================================================================================
FAILED tests/e2e/test_e2e_lifecycle_consistency.py::test_cross_shard_complex_update - RuntimeError: Error in clear operation: No strategy supports item of type NoneType: None. Available strategies: ['NPUTensorKVClientAdapter', 'GeneralKVClientAdapter']
================================================================================== 1 failed, 7 passed, 1 warning in 17.76s ===================================================================================
```
- `clear_partition` still raises error in
```test_e2e_lifecycle_consistency.py::test_cross_shard_complex_update```,
because some keys generated by metadata were not put before.
- We plan to support automatic startup of **Yuanrong** for `tq.init()`.

---------

Signed-off-by: dpj135 <958208521@qq.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use PrepareForBuildDependsOn to ensure wwwroot/cgscript-cm6.js is built before static web asset discovery

The Razor SDK's static web asset pipeline requires the wwwroot/ file to exist
before ResolveProjectStaticWebAssets and ResolveCurrentProjectStaticWebAssetsInputs
run. PrepareForBuildDependsOn runs our BuildNpmBundle target early enough (before
any build targets evaluate @(Content)) to satisfy this requirement.

Also use a relative Content item path ('wwwroot\cgscript-cm6.js') instead of an
absolute path so ResolveCurrentProjectStaticWebAssetsInputs correctly picks it up
(it filters @(Content) by identity starting with 'wwwroot/').

This restores the staticwebassets/ and build/*.props structure in the nupkg that
was present in v2.20.1 but lost when wwwroot/cgscript-cm6.js was gitignored.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: desktop mode with electron-mcp RPC/MCP on port 18101

Architecture:
  cicy-code --desktop
    → Go API server (port 18008)
    → electron (official binary) + electron-mcp JS (port 18101)
    → Electron window loads http://localhost:18008/?token=xxx

Key design: uses official Electron binary (npm i -g electron),
no code signing needed. electron-mcp provides RPC/MCP server
with 50+ tools (screenshot, automation, clipboard, CDP, etc).

Go backend:
  - desktop.go: ensureDesktop(), proxy /api/desktop/* → 18101
  - /api/desktop/status: desktop mode info + tool list
  - /api/desktop/rpc/*: proxy to electron-mcp RPC endpoints
  - /api/desktop/mcp: proxy to MCP SSE endpoint

npm bin:
  - --desktop flag: start API → wait health → launch electron
  - Discovery: global electron + global cicy → bundled desktop/
  - Auto-install electron and cicy if missing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: update desktop submodule to 7ef8161

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- npx cicy-code --desktop: start API → wait ready → launch Electron
- Electron opens http://localhost:18008/?token=xxx via --url flag
- Discovery order: global 'cicy' CLI → bundled desktop/ → npx cicy

cmd.Env was redundant since users always have our tmux.conf.

is the correct solution. The send-keys approach was belt-and-suspenders
that caused visible 'export TERM=xterm-256color' noise in terminals.

fix: add json tags to NotificationRow for correct API serialization

Without json tags, Go serialized struct fields as PascalCase (Title, GroupType,
IsRead, CreatedAt) but mobile expected snake_case (title, group_type, is_read,
createdAt). This caused all notification fields to appear blank in the inbox.

fix: set TERM=xterm-256color on PTY child process

Root cause: exec.Command inherits parent process TERM which may be
empty/dumb when started via nohup, causing 'terminal does not support
clear' in browser terminal.

Fix: explicitly set TERM=xterm-256color in cmd.Env before pty.Start().

Expose boss data and benchmark-based win estimates: update README with GitHub link, instructions, and explanation of how Boss Result is calculated. feat: add telemetry instrumentation for Copilot agent flows (#7199)

* feat: add telemetry instrumentation for Copilot agent flows

Add OpenTelemetry spans and usage attributes to track Copilot agent
session lifecycle, initialization prompts, message usage, and consent
decisions. All instrumentation is in the core agent packages so it
works for both direct CLI and gRPC extension framework consumers.

Changes:
- Define 16 new AttributeKey fields in internal/tracing/fields/fields.go
  covering session (id, isNew, messageCount), init prompts (isFirstRun,
  reasoningEffort, model, consentScope), per-message metrics (model,
  tokens, billingRate, premiumRequests, durationMs), and consent counts
  (approvedCount, deniedCount)
- Add copilot.initialize span in CopilotAgent.Initialize() tracking
  reasoning level, model selection, and isFirstRun
- Add copilot.message span in CopilotAgent.SendMessage() tracking
  per-message usage metrics and cumulative message count
- Add copilot.session span in ensureSession() tracking session
  creation vs resumption with hashed session ID
- Track consent approved/denied counts in permission handler and
  record as usage attributes on agent Stop()
- Track workflow consent scope selection in PromptWorkflowConsent()
- Rename InitMethod from 'agent' to 'copilot' in cmd/init.go
- Add InitMethod='environment' for previously untracked init branch
- Record aggregate copilot metrics as usage attributes in initAppWithAgent

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: address PR feedback on copilot telemetry

- Register event names in tracing/events/events.go (CopilotInitializeEvent,
  CopilotSessionEvent) per convention
- Remove per-message copilot.message span — too chatty; emit only
  session-level metrics
- Consolidate all cumulative metrics into Stop() (mode, messageCount,
  tokens, billing, consent counts, session ID) so telemetry is captured
  from the core agent package regardless of caller
- Remove duplicate aggregate metrics from cmd/init.go — now handled
  by agent.Stop()
- Use defer + named returns in Initialize() and ensureSession() to
  capture errors via span.EndWithStatus(err) and reduce duplication
- Log resumeSessionID in ensureSession defer even on early failure

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- LAB-03: Multiple resources — implicit/explicit dependencies, lifecycle meta-args

commit.message:
Remove auto-labels from issue templates

The bug_report, submit-a-request, and submit-a-design-proposal issue
templates currently auto-apply 'bug' and 'enhancement' labels. This
causes issues to arrive pre-labeled with types that may not be accurate,
making triage harder since the template-applied labels can't be trusted.

Removing auto-labels ensures all type classification happens during
triage, giving the team confidence that labeled issues have been
reviewed.

fix: resolve principal ID in resource tenant for preflight role check (#7174)

* fix: resolve principal ID in resource tenant for preflight role check

For B2B/guest users, CurrentPrincipalId returns the home-tenant object ID,
which differs from the guest identity in the resource tenant. The
assignedTo() role assignment filter cannot resolve cross-tenant identities,
causing false positive permission warnings during preflight.

This tactical fix resolves the principal ID via Graph /me against the
subscription's resource tenant (sub.TenantId) instead of the user-access
tenant (sub.UserAccessTenantId), so the role assignment query uses the
correct guest identity. Falls back to the default provider on failure.

Also adds 'Role Based Access Control Administrator' to the warning message
as a valid role for the required permission.

Fixes #7169
See #7173 for the broader CurrentPrincipalId fix across all callers.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* address PR #7174 review feedback

- Fix per-action conditional tracking: track unconditional grants per
  required action instead of a single global flag, so a later
  unconditional role clears the conditional flag even when a conditional
  role granted the same action first (JeffreyCA, wbreza)

- Return PermissionCheckResult by value instead of pointer: idiomatic
  for a 2-bool struct, eliminates nil checks and GC pressure (wbreza)

- Add /* tenantID */ comment on empty string constants passed to
  newAzdCredential call sites in manager.go (tg-msft)

- Enhance credential test spy to verify expected tenant ID value, not
  just option count (wbreza)

- Add 3 new tests for checkActionsFromRoles: conditional-then-unconditional
  ordering, all-conditional, and multi-action mixed conditionality

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

commit.message:
ci: adjust minimum Python version in broad charm compatibility tests (#2317)

A large number of our [broad charm compatibility
tests](https://github.com/canonical/operator/actions/workflows/published-charms-tests.yaml)
fail because the charm declares that it works with Python 3.8. That
conflicts with us pushing the latest ops 3.x into the dependencies.

Generally, if we've made a change to ops[...] that breaks charms, that
will be across Python versions, and we're more concerned with 3.10+
anyway (and in main, we're only supporting 3.10+). We could have some
compatibility tests that check the latest 2.x branch for charms that
expect 3.8, but that doesn't seem as useful.

This PR adjusts the dependency patching to also handle bumping the
Python version to 3.10. It [reduces the number of failures to around
6](https://github.com/tonyandrewmeyer/operator/actions/runs/23189775456),
some of which are only for static checks (which I haven't worked on in
this PR at all).

Since the changes are more complex now, they are moved out of the action
file (YAML+bash) and into a helper (Python) script. Primarily, we do the
work through tox, which I found to be the most consistent way to handle
things across packaging tools (particularly given that we are using
wheels now rather than git references).

The script also adds support for `tox.toml`, as some charms use that
rather than `tox.ini`.

---------

commit.message:
Simplify share: clipboard + gh-slack only

Replace generic webhook support with gh-slack integration.
If gh-slack is installed and a channel is configured in
~/.config/gh-games/config.toml, players can post results
directly to Slack. Otherwise, clipboard copy is always available.

Config format:
  [share]
  slack_channel = "fun-games"
